title: LLM Wiki
summary: LLM-maintained wiki compounds knowledge; RAG re-derives it every query Three-layer architecture: raw sources, wiki, schema
creator_view: LLM-maintained wiki compounds knowledge; RAG re-derives it every query Three-layer architecture: raw sources, wiki, schema

candidates.name: Three-layer architecture pattern for LLM knowledge bases
candidates.description:
Raw sources (immutable) -> Wiki (LLM-owned) -> Schema (co-evolved config). Clean separation where human curates and LLM maintains.

Extraction action: Compare against SoNash's current architecture (.research/ = raw sources, docs/ + MEMORY.md = wiki layer, CLAUDE.md = schema). Identify gaps.

candidates.name: Ingest-Query-Lint operational triad
candidates.description:
Three operations that keep a knowledge base healthy: ingest new sources, query with compounding answers, lint for contradictions/orphans/staleness.

Extraction action: Map to existing skills: /repo-analysis + /website-analysis = ingest, /deep-research = query, orphan detection + /alerts = lint. Identify missing lint operations.

candidates.name: Answers-compound-into-wiki principle
candidates.description:
Good query answers should be filed back as wiki pages. Explorations compound alongside ingested sources. Prevents knowledge loss to chat history.

Extraction action: Evaluate: do /deep-research outputs, /brainstorm sessions, and /deep-plan decisions persist as reusable knowledge? Or do they die in .research/ archives?

candidates.name: Index + Log dual navigation system
candidates.description:
Content-oriented index (catalog with summaries) + chronological log (append-only, parseable). Works without embedding infrastructure at moderate scale.

Extraction action: Compare: DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md = index, SESSION_HISTORY.md + commit-log.jsonl = log, research-index.jsonl = research index. What's missing?

candidates.name: qmd local markdown search (MCP + CLI)
candidates.description:
BM25/vector hybrid search for markdown files with LLM re-ranking. Both CLI and MCP server. github.com/tobi/qmd.

Extraction action: Evaluate for JASON-OS search layer. Currently using Grep + DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md. At 1000+ docs, may need proper search.

site.title: LLM Wiki
site.description: A pattern for building personal knowledge bases using LLMs
site.author: Andrej Karpathy

absence_patterns: No benchmarks or quantitative comparisons (RAG vs wiki approach)
No discussion of context window limits or scaling beyond ~hundreds of pages
No mention of multi-user collaboration patterns
No discussion of version control conflicts when LLM + human both edit
No treatment of structured data (databases, APIs) as sources
No security/privacy considerations for sensitive knowledge bases
key_claims: LLM-maintained wiki compounds knowledge; RAG re-derives it every query
Three-layer architecture: raw sources, wiki, schema
Index.md + log.md sufficient at ~100 sources / hundreds of pages
Humans abandon wikis because maintenance burden grows faster than value
LLMs solve the Memex maintenance problem Vannevar Bush couldn't
Schema (CLAUDE.md) is the key configuration that makes the LLM disciplined
Good answers should be filed back into wiki as new pages
